title: Automated Fare Collection (AFC) Smart Card Transaction Data

dataset_schema.label: AFC Transaction Records
dataset_schema.description: One row per tap event. Fields: anonymised card ID, boarding stop/station, alighting stop (only in closed systems), boarding timestamp, alighting timestamp, fare paid, route/line ID.

dataset_schema.label: GTFS (General Transit Feed Specification)
dataset_schema.description: Timetable and network topology: stop locations (lat/lon), routes, schedules, headways.

original_intent.label: AFC system
original_intent.description: Revenue collection, fare enforcement, blacklisting lost cards, financial settlement between operators, and ridership reporting. Schema reflects financial and ticketing needs, not travel behaviour research.

original_intent.label: GTFS
original_intent.description: Published for third-party journey planning apps and regulatory reporting.

repurposed_use: Replaces costly household travel surveys and studies the impact of fare changes or disruptions. Provides standard inputs to transit network planning and investment decisions.

limitations.label: schema incompleteness
limitations.description: Boarding-only systems (most buses, NYC subway) do not record alighting. Destination must be inferred via trip-chaining, achieving only ~70-75% accuracy for irregular travellers.

limitations.label: omitted variables
limitations.description: No trip purpose, age, income, or disability status. All sociodemographic and behavioural context must be inferred indirectly or supplemented with survey data.

limitations.label: temporal instability
limitations.description: Fare structures, route IDs, and card hashing conventions change over time. Longitudinal analysis requires careful schema harmonisation, especially after network reforms.

transformations.label: feature engineering
transformations.description: Alighting stop inferred for boarding-only systems via trip-chaining

transformations.label: data integration
transformations.description: AFC stop IDs joined to GTFS stops.txt to assign lat/lon coordinates, enabling spatial aggregation to zones or traffic analysis areas for OD matrix construction.

transformations.label: aggregation
transformations.description: Inferred trip origins and destinations aggregated from card level to zone-pair counts.

transformations.label: discretisation
transformations.description: Boarding timestamps classified into time-of-day periods (AM peak, inter-peak, PM peak, off-peak) and days of week to enable temporal segmentation of travel patterns.

transformations.label: normalisation
transformations.description: Card-level trip frequencies normalised across observation periods to distinguish regular commuters from occasional or irregular users before clustering.
